page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and is equipped with a 320 MB L3 cache. Memory resources consist of 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 2500 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score and text processing performance ranking in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25% tier. However, multi-core scaling presents a notable tradeoff; both stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small blocks but drops to the bottom 25% tier for uncached large block reads. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput align with the middle 50% average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage and DDR5 memory relative to its 4 vCPU compute footprint. This resource balance makes it highly suitable for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. Ideal workloads include relational databases, NoSQL data stores, caching layers, and text processing applications. Conversely, the weak multi-core scaling and uncached memory read bandwidth make it less optimal for highly parallelized compute tasks or large-scale software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory and a bundled 2500 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. However, multi-core scaling and uncached large-block memory reads represent performance tradeoffs, landing in the bottom 25% tier. The instance provides high storage and memory density relative to its compute capacity, offering qualitative cost efficiency for data-heavy applications. It is best suited for databases, caching layers, and text processing workloads that benefit from fast local storage and strong single-threaded execution rather than massive parallel compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2500 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense, memory-optimized instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and strong single-core processing for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 320 MB L3 cache
Bundled 2500 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
32.0 GB of fast 6 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and text processing
